title: 10 best herbal teas for nail fungus
intro_desc: Herbal teas for nail fungus are infusions made from plants that have antifungal properties, which can help to treat and prevent the recurrence of nail fungal infections. Examples of herbal teas that can be used include those made from calendula, lavender, and oregano. These teas can be consumed internally to boost the immune system and promote overall health, as well as used topically by soaking the affected nails in the tea. By using herbal teas, individuals can treat nail fungus in a natural and holistic way, improving their quality of life by avoiding the potential side effects of conventional antifungal treatments. Additionally, herbal teas can be a cost-effective and accessible option for those looking for a more natural approach to treating nail fungus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea helps with nail fungus because it contains powerful antifungal and antibacterial properties. The herbal tea is made from the bark of the Melaleuca squamea tree, which is native to Australia. The bark contains various compounds, including terpinen-4-ol, that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi. Regular consumption of this tea can help to reduce the symptoms of nail fungus, such as discoloration, thickening, and crumbling of the nails. Furthermore, the anti-inflammatory properties of the tea can help to alleviate any pain or discomfort associated with the infection. Overall, narrow-leaved paperbark tea offers a natural and effective way to treat nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal properties: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea contains active constituents such as terpinen-4-ol, which is important for nail fungus as it inhibits the growth of fungal cells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, such as alpha-pinene, that can help reduce swelling and redness around the infected nail, providing relief from the discomfort caused by the fungus.
Immune system support: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea contains antioxidants and other compounds that help boost the immune system, allowing the body to better fight off the nail fungus infection.
Wound healing properties: The tea has active constituents, including eucalyptol, that promote wound healing, which can help the body repair damaged nail tissue caused by the fungal infection.

Antimicrobial properties: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary bacterial or fungal infections, ensuring that the treated area heals properly and reducing the risk of recurring infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 narrow-leaved paperbark (Melaleuca squamea) leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly under cold running water, then air dry or pat gently with a clean towel.
Crush or tear the leaves into small pieces, place them in a pot, and add 500ml of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea, let it cool down, and drink 2-3 cups daily, preferably after meals, for at least one month to treat nail fungus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with nail fungus because it contains powerful antifungal and antiseptic properties, thanks to its high concentration of thymol. This compound has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, making it an effective natural remedy for treating nail fungus. Regular consumption of thyme tea can help strengthen the immune system, allowing the body to better fight off fungal infections. Moreover, thyme tea can be applied topically to the affected nails, providing direct antifungal action and helping to reduce inflammation and irritation. Its potent antioxidant properties also promote nail health and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Thyme tea has antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail fungus, due to the presence of thymol and other volatile oils.
Fungicidal properties: Thyme tea has fungicidal properties that can kill fungi causing nail fungus, thanks to the active constituent thymol.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with nail fungus, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin and nails from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate nail fungus, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other polyphenols.

Antibacterial properties: Thyme tea has antibacterial properties that can help prevent secondary bacterial infections, which can occur as a result of nail fungus, thanks to the presence of thymol and other volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used thyme leaves, and allow it to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) for added flavor and potential antifungal benefits.
Drink the thyme tea once or twice a day, ideally after meals, for several weeks to help treat nail fungus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with nail fungus because it contains powerful antifungal and antibacterial properties, primarily due to its high concentration of thymol. This compound has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and help eradicate existing infections. Regular consumption of oregano tea can help strengthen your immune system, allowing your body to better combat and prevent nail fungus. Furthermore, applying oregano tea topically to affected nails can provide direct antifungal relief, potentially aiding in the restoration of healthy nail growth.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Oregano tea contains thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for nail fungus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Oregano tea can help reduce inflammation and redness around the infected nail, providing relief from discomfort caused by nail fungus.
Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial properties of oregano tea, due to its thymol and carvacrol content, can help prevent secondary bacterial infections associated with nail fungus.
Immune system support: Oregano tea can help boost the immune system, which is essential for fighting off and preventing the spread of nail fungus.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in oregano tea, such as rosmarinic acid, can help protect nail tissue from oxidative stress and support overall nail health.
remedy_recipe: In a stovepot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried oregano leaves, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and let the oregano leaves steep for another 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the oregano leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak your nails in the oregano tea for 15-20 minutes, then rinse and dry your nails thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with nail fungus because it contains powerful antifungal and antiseptic properties, thanks to its high concentration of terpinen-4-ol. This compound is known to inhibit the growth of fungi, making it an effective natural remedy for treating nail fungus. The tea can be applied directly to the affected area or consumed as a beverage to help boost the immune system and promote overall health. Regular consumption or application of Tasmanian blue gum tea can help reduce the symptoms of nail fungus, such as discoloration, thickening, and brittleness of the nails.
remedy_properties: Fungicidal properties: Tasmanian blue gum tea contains terpinen-4-ol, which has been shown to have fungicidal effects, helping to kill the fungus causing nail infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of globulol and viridiflorol in Tasmanian blue gum tea helps reduce inflammation, providing relief from the redness and swelling associated with nail fungus.

Antimicrobial properties: The tea's high concentration of α-pinene and limonene exhibits antimicrobial effects, aiding in preventing the spread of nail fungus and inhibiting secondary bacterial infections.
Immune-boosting properties: Tasmanian blue gum tea is rich in 1,8-cineole, which has been shown to boost the immune system, helping the body fight off fungal infections more effectively.
Wound-healing properties: The presence of α-terpineol in the tea promotes wound healing, assisting in the growth of new, healthy nail tissue and reducing the likelihood of fungal reinfection.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves, allowing them to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove leaves, discarding the solid remains.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak your nails in the tea for 15-20 minutes, making sure they are fully submerged.
Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with nail fungus because it contains antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help inhibit the growth of fungi causing the infection. The active compounds in rosemary, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosol, have been shown to have antifungal effects against various types of fungi, including those that cause nail infections. Moreover, rosemary tea is rich in antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and promote healing. By drinking rosemary tea regularly, the active compounds can be absorbed into the bloodstream, helping to fight the fungal infection from within. However, it is important to note that while rosemary tea may help alleviate nail fungus, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe or persistent cases.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Rosemary tea has antifungal properties that help to inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail fungus, due to the presence of active compounds such as camphor, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and redness associated with nail fungus, thanks to the presence of compounds such as carnosol and carnosic acid.

Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea has antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin and nails from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate nail fungus, due to the presence of active compounds such as rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Antibacterial properties: Rosemary tea has antibacterial properties that help to prevent secondary bacterial infections that can occur as a result of nail fungus, thanks to the presence of active compounds such as camphor and eucalyptol.

Circulation-boosting properties: Rosemary tea has circulation-boosting properties that help to improve blood flow to the nails, promoting healing and preventing the spread of nail fungus, due to the presence of active compounds such as 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves, letting it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off the heat and allow the mixture to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the rosemary leaves, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, consistently, for best results in treating nail fungus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tea helps with nail fungus because it has antifungal properties that can help to inhibit the growth of fungi causing the infection. The main active ingredient in garlic, allicin, has been shown to have antifungal effects against a variety of fungi, including those that can cause nail infections. Drinking garlic tea can help to deliver these antifungal compounds directly to the site of the infection, where they can help to eliminate the fungi and promote healthy nail growth. Additionally, garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with nail fungus. Overall, incorporating garlic tea into your daily routine can be a natural and effective way to support nail health and combat fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Fungicidal properties: Garlic tea has the ability to inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail fungus, due to the presence of allicin, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal properties.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic tea can help reduce inflammation associated with nail fungus, providing relief from symptoms such as redness and swelling, thanks to its content of diallyl disulfide and s-allyl cysteine.

Antimicrobial properties: Garlic tea can help prevent the spread of nail fungus by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms, due to the presence of ajoene, a compound that has been shown to have antimicrobial properties.

Immune-boosting properties: Garlic tea can help strengthen the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off nail fungus, due to its rich content of vitamin C, selenium, and other antioxidants.

Antioxidant properties: Garlic tea's high antioxidant content can help protect the body from the damage caused by free radicals, which can weaken the immune system and make it more susceptible to nail fungus, due to the presence of flavonoids and other antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: Peel and finely chop 3 cloves of garlic, let sit for 10 minutes to activate allicin.
In a pot, combine 2 cups of water, chopped garlic, and 1 tablespoon of dried oregano or thyme.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the garlic and herbs.
Drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day, until symptoms improve.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with nail fungus because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory compound. This compound inhibits the growth of fungi, including the ones causing nail fungus. Moreover, turmeric's antioxidant properties help boost the immune system, enabling the body to fight off the infection more effectively. Regular consumption of turmeric tea can help reduce the symptoms of nail fungus, such as yellowing, thickening, and crumbling of the nails. Furthermore, its soothing effect helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with the infection.
remedy_properties: Fungicidal properties: Turmeric tea has the ability to kill fungi, making it effective in treating nail fungus, due to the presence of curcumin and other curcuminoids.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric tea can reduce inflammation and swelling associated with nail fungus, as curcumin inhibits the production of inflammatory chemicals.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric tea's antioxidants, especially curcumin, help neutralize free radicals, promoting tissue repair and reducing damage caused by nail fungus.
Antimicrobial properties: Turmeric tea can inhibit the growth of microorganisms, including fungi, bacteria, and viruses, which helps prevent the spread of nail fungus and supports healing.
Immune-boosting properties: Turmeric tea can enhance the immune system, aiding the body in fighting off and preventing nail fungus, due to curcumin's ability to modulate the immune response.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water, 1 teaspoon of ground turmeric, and 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/2 teaspoon of apple cider vinegar.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soak onto your nails for at least 15 minutes before rinsing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with nail fungus because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have antifungal properties. This compound can help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, promoting healthier nails over time. Additionally, licorice tea is also known for its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which can help the body to fight off fungal infections more effectively. By drinking licorice tea regularly, individuals may be able to improve the health of their nails and reduce the severity of fungal infections. However, it's important to note that licorice tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, and anyone experiencing symptoms of nail fungus should consult with a healthcare provider.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Licorice tea contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, making it effective in treating nail fungus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice tea can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with nail fungus, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Immune-boosting properties: Licorice tea contains compounds that can help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off nail fungus infections.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the body against damage from free radicals, which can weaken the nails and make them more susceptible to fungal infections.
Detoxifying properties: Licorice tea can help support the body's natural detoxification processes, which can help eliminate toxins that can contribute to the development of nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak your nails in the tea for 15-20 minutes, then rinse and dry your nails thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with nail fungus because it has antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can help inhibit the growth of fungi causing the infection. The active compounds in sage, such as sage oil and camphor, have been shown to have antifungal effects against various types of fungi, including those responsible for nail fungus. By drinking sage tea, the active compounds can be absorbed into the bloodstream and reach the affected nails, helping to eliminate the fungal infection and promote healthy nail growth. Additionally, sage tea can help boost the immune system, which can further aid in fighting off the infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sage tea has antifungal properties that help to combat the growth of fungus causing nail infections, due to the presence of active constituents such as safficinolide and sageone.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with nail fungus, thanks to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of sage tea help to protect nail tissue from oxidative stress and support the immune system in fighting off fungal infections, due to the high levels of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other antioxidant compounds.
Astringent properties: Sage tea's astringent properties help to dry out excess moisture and create an unfavorable environment for fungal growth, due to the tannins present in the tea.

Wound healing properties: Sage tea promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, helping to repair damaged nail tissue and restore its healthy appearance, thanks to the presence of essential oils, flavonoids, and other active constituents.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the sage leaves and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the sage leaves.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day, for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with nail fungus because it contains powerful antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can effectively combat and eliminate the fungal infection causing nail fungus. The tea is made from the fennel plant's seeds, which are rich in compounds such as trans-anethole, fenchone, and estragole, known for their potent antifungal activities. These compounds work by disrupting the cell membrane of the fungi, thereby inhibiting their growth and spread. Moreover, fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation around the infected nail, promoting faster healing and recovery. Regular consumption of fennel tea, combined with proper nail care, can significantly improve the condition of nail fungus and prevent its recurrence.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Fennel tea contains compounds such as trans-anethole, fenchone, and estragole that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for nail fungus.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tea's ability to reduce inflammation can help alleviate symptoms associated with nail fungus, such as redness, swelling, and pain.
Antioxidant property: Fennel tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect nails from oxidative stress and support the immune system in fighting off fungal infections.
Antimicrobial property: Fennel tea's antimicrobial properties can help prevent secondary bacterial infections that may occur as a result of nail fungus.
Diuretic property: Fennel tea's diuretic effect can help eliminate toxins from the body, promoting overall health and supporting the immune system in combating nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of crushed fennel seeds, simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover and let the mixture steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the fennel seeds.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak your nails in the fennel tea for 15-20 minutes, then rinse and dry thoroughly.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nail fungus with herbal teas is by soaking the affected nails in a tea bath. Use potent antifungal herbs such as calendula, oregano, and tea tree. Steep 1-2 tablespoons of the dried herb in boiling water for 15-20 minutes. Let the tea cool down to a warm temperature, then soak the nails for 15-20 minutes, twice a day. Consistency is key for successful treatment; continue this routine for several months to ensure the fungus is completely eliminated.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nail fungus that are treated with herbal teas include certain skin and nail infections. For instance, green tea has been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions like athlete's foot or ringworm. Chamomile tea is another option, known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe skin irritations and infections. Additionally, research suggests that certain herbal teas, like those made from calendula or echinacea, may possess antifungal properties that could aid in treating nail fungus or other fungal infections. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
